content.problemStatement:
There are NNN circles in a plane. Circle iii and circle jjj form a good pair if it is possible to choose a point P1P_1P1тАЛ on the perimeter of circle iii and a point P2P_2P2тАЛ on the perimeter of circle jjj such that the Euclidean distance between P1P_1P1тАЛ and P2P_2P2тАЛ is exactly KKK. (Note that P1P_1P1тАЛ and P2P_2P2тАЛ do not need to have integer coordinates.)

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given the required distance KKK. Chef is interested in finding the number of good pairs of distinct circles for each query. Please help him.

Note: Circle iii and circle jjj are distinct when iтЙаji \neq jiюАа=j. There may be any number of circles that coincide (have identical centers and radii).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ denoting the number of circles and the number of queries respectively.
Each of the following NNN lines contains three space-separated integers XXX, YYY and RRR describing a circle with radius RRR and center (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y).
Each of the next QQQ lines contains one integer KKK describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer тАФ the number of good pairs of circles.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The distance between the point (0.00,тИТ5.00)(0.00, -5.00)(0.00,тИТ5.00) on circle 111 and the point (8.00,1.00)(8.00, 1.00)(8.00,1.00) on circle 222 is 101010. There is no pair of points on these two circles with distance 000 or 202020.
